title: MailerLite vs Brevo (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Brevo: pricing, features, and performance. Find the best email marketing tool for your business needs.
h1: MailerLite vs Brevo: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: MailerLite and Brevo are two popular email marketing platforms with free tiers, but they serve different needs. MailerLite excels at simplicity and e-commerce, while Brevo offers broader multi-channel capabilities including SMS, WhatsApp, and CRM. This guide helps you choose the right platform based on your business requirements.
choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer wanting a simple, budget-friendly email marketing tool
You need landing pages, pop-ups, and strong e-commerce integrations
You prefer an intuitive interface with minimal complexity and learning curve
choose_b_if: You need a comprehensive CRM platform with multi-channel marketing (email, SMS, WhatsApp)
You require transactional email API capabilities or live chat support
You want the largest free tier to grow your subscriber list without paying initially

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. MailerLite excels for simple email marketing and e-commerce businesses, with better user ratings and easier setup. Brevo wins for businesses needing multi-channel marketing, CRM features, and transactional email capabilities. Choose based on your specific requirements.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Brevo?
faq.a: MailerLite has a lower paid entry point at $9/month versus Brevo's $25/month. However, Brevo offers a significantly larger free tier (100,000 vs 1,000 subscribers), making it more cost-effective if you don't need paid features immediately. Long-term costs depend on your subscriber count and feature needs.

faq.q: Can I switch from Brevo to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list exports. You can export your contacts and subscriber data from Brevo and import them into MailerLite. Most integrations will need to be reconfigured in your new platform, but the migration process is straightforward for most users.
